summary: Frontend-only testing of (a) Sunnah-correct dua_eat + hadith refs in 6 existing duas, (b) 5 new daily duas, (c) brand-new Real-time Prayer Mode at #prayermode. Prayer Mode passes 100% (all 5 prayer selectors, timer 3-18s countdown, step progression 1/11→2/11, pause/stop/TTS, back nav, PL/EN headers). Content-data is correct in source. ONE bug: the 5 new lessons + 6 existing 'Islam – codzienne'/'Islam – Daily' duas are filtered OUT of the #lessons UI by an explicit filter in lessons() and they are not displayed elsewhere either.

ui_bugs.issue: An explicit filter `const islamicCats = ['Islam','Islam –']; lessonsData = allData.filter(l => !islamicCats.some(p => l.category.startsWith(p)))` removes ALL lessons whose category starts with 'Islam'. Result: dua_eat, dua_after_eat, dua_sleep, dua_wake, dua_mirror, dua_stress + 5 new (dua_toilet_in/out, dua_after_wudu, dua_to_mosque, dua_morning_evening) are never rendered in #lessons in either PL or EN. The review request asserts they should appear in the 'Islam – codzienne'/'Islam – Daily' category — they do NOT.

integration_issues.issue: duasView() at line 4585 uses a separate `expandedDuas` data source and does NOT consume LESSONS_DATA — so the 5 new duas in LESSONS_DATA are also invisible at #duas. They are only reachable through the global search box (LESSONS_DATA is searched at line 3823).

action_items: Fix the lessons() filter so 'Islam – codzienne'/'Islam – Daily' lessons display. Either (a) remove the filter and let the new duas show as a category card, or (b) merge them into duasView() / a dedicated daily-duas section, or (c) keep the filter but add a separate data-testid section in #lessons or #islam that lists the daily duas. Whichever path is chosen, the 11 daily duas (6 existing + 5 new) need a visible UI surface so users can read the Arabic, transliteration, meaning + hadith refs.
critical_code_review_comments: app.js is now 5039 lines — repeating the same recommendation from iterations 1 & 2: split into modules (lessons.js, prayer.js, duas.js, prayermode.js, badges.js).

LESSONS_DATA contains 'Islam – codzienne'/'Islam – Daily' categories that are filtered out by the lessons() view itself — this is a code smell. Either don't seed Islam categories into LESSONS_DATA, or render them in a dedicated tab/section.

duasView() uses a separate `expandedDuas` array that overlaps in concept with the dua_* entries in LESSONS_DATA. Two parallel sources for the same domain object causes drift and bugs like the one above. Recommend consolidating to a single source.
Tailwind via cdn.tailwindcss.com still produces production warning (unchanged from prior iterations).

Prayer Mode timer relies on `prayerInfo.durations` per language — values look reasonable (3-18s). Note: 11 steps × 2 rakahs ≈ 2-4 minutes total in test mode, but real prayer should be ~3-5 minutes. Consider exposing a 'real-time vs guide-time' toggle so durations can match a real Maghrib (~3-5 min) without rushing the user.
Web Speech API in pmodeSpeakCurrent is fired without checking `'speechSynthesis' in window`. In some restricted browsers/iframes this could throw — recommend a guard.

success_rate.frontend: 94% (16/17 review-request items pass; lessons-display item fails)

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this PWA
context_for_next_testing_agent: Prayer Mode (#prayermode) is fully verified end-to-end via Playwright: all 5 [data-testid='pmode-select-{Fajr|Dhuhr|Asr|Maghrib|Isha}'] buttons exist, Maghrib click sets rakah display to 1/3, timer-circle shows '--' initially, Start enables pause and starts countdown (saw '3' then '16' as it advanced through takbir then qiyam), step-display advanced 1/11→2/11 within 5s, Pause holds the number for 2.2s without change, Stop resets to '--' and 1/11, TTS click does not crash. EN lang toggle correctly renders 'Real-time Prayer Mode' header. CTA howtopray-go-prayermode-btn navigates to #prayermode. Back button (pmode-back-howtopray) navigates to #howtopray. NO JS errors during navigation across #prayermode/#lessons/#howtopray/#islam/#home/#duas. Content data (dua_eat, 6 existing dua refs, 5 new daily duas) is CORRECT in /app/frontend/src/app.js source — confirmed via grep at lines 218-234 (PL) and 279-295 (EN). The single remaining failure is a UI display issue: lessons() explicitly filters out 'Islam' categories so the new duas don't render in #lessons. After fix, retest #lessons (PL+EN) for the 5 new titles + dua_eat refs.
rca_of_the_issue: The lessons() function at /app/frontend/src/app.js:2151-2153 contains: `const islamicCats=['Islam','Islam –']; const lessonsData = allData.filter(l => !islamicCats.some(prefix => l.category.startsWith(prefix)));`. This filter is applied BEFORE the category cards are built (line 2154: `categories=[...new Set(lessonsData.map(l=>l.category))]`), so any lesson whose category starts with 'Islam' is silently dropped from both the category card list and the lesson grid. The new duas were added with category 'Islam – codzienne' / 'Islam – Daily' so they hit this filter. Additionally, duasView() (line 4585) uses a different data source `expandedDuas`, so they're not displayed there either. Reproduction: open https://alif-quran-learn.preview.emergentagent.com/#lessons → click 'Lekcje'/'Lessons' sub-tab → only Basics/Family/Colors/Numbers/Food/Time/'Poland & Indonesia' categories appear; no 'Islam – codzienne' card. Mitigation: remove the filter (simplest), OR render Islam-prefixed lessons in a dedicated section in #lessons or as cards in #duas / #islam. Given that the review request's intent is for users to discover these daily duas, the simplest fix is to drop the filter so the new 'Islam – codzienne' / 'Islam – Daily' category card appears alongside Basics, Family, etc.
